reviews.review_msg: Really just unbearable. Nice guy and passionate but super monotone, lectures are hard to follow and lack clear direction, and there are zero assignments in the course aside from a group project. Would not have taken if not required. Sad that a 400 is the worst/ most boring class at SFU CMNS.

I hate attending lectures but Dr. Laba is one of the best professors I've ever had. He is a gem of a human, he's caring, encouraging and truly wants the best for his students. He's so woke and cool, I wish  I could take a class with him again!

Lectures were interesting and he's overall a likeable, chill guy who has a lot of passion for what he teaches. He is interested in his students and gives great feedback and advice. Yes, there's a bit of writing, but barely as much as most comms courses.

Absolute gem of a professor, gives very detailed feedback while trying to fit into your perspective of the topic, even if he disagrees with your perspective. He's very well versed, passionate about the course and genuinely wants you to succeed.

Prof Martin is one of the best prof u will ever encouter in SFU. He understands his student's need and his lecture is very entertaining and never dull. I can listen to him talk all day and never get bored as he is very knowledgable in the field with many great stories about his life. Overall hundred percent recommand anyone to take his class.

I absolutely loved this course! Martin Laba is a phenomenal prof. So engaging, interesting, and inspirational. I suggest everyone to take this course, even if you are not a cmns student. The grading is also very fair.

Martin is by far the best professor at SFU. The project you create in this class is relevant and something you could add to your resume. Readings - don't bother, and in lecture he just talks about current events and the history of PR - very interesting. LOVE HIM. TAKE THIS COURSE. SO INSPIRING.

Dr. Laba is definitely passionate and cares a lot about his students. He's interesting to listen to but I felt like the course didn't have a clear course plan/trajectory. We talked about politics or other topics a lot, the readings felt irrelevant to me. I like Martin but in terms of actual learning, I didn't gain much in this class.

Martin might be my favourite prof at SFU. His lectures are engaging - he encourages students to weigh in and he'll love you if you participate. The class deals with real social issues in a hands-on way, creating campaigns and giving presentations. He cares about his students and wants you to succeed. I've enjoyed all of my classes with him.

the class was ok, but didn't offer anything really new in the frame of media and society, department are somewhat repetitive. martin is all personality, less substance, a bit out of touch. nearing the end of his teaching career but acts more like a 20something with his hipster clothes and talk, plus loves attention. office hours good
